title: Writing - Part 1: Local vs Global Business
description: Your class has been discussing whether people should support local businesses rather than large international chains.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the arguments in your notes. You should explain which argument you find most convincing, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Money spent in local shops stays within the community and supports local jobs.
International chains often offer lower prices and greater variety, which benefits consumers.
Local businesses typically have smaller carbon footprints than large corporations with global supply chains.

structure.intro: Introduce the debate between local and global business and state the two arguments you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first argument and the evidence that supports it.
structure.body2: Discuss the second argument and compare its strength to the first.
structure.conclusion: State which argument you find most convincing and explain your overall view.

content.modelAnswer:
The rise of large international corporations has transformed the way people shop, prompting debate about whether consumers should make a conscious effort to support local businesses instead. Two compelling arguments in favour of buying locally are the economic benefit to communities and the lower environmental impact.

When money is spent at local shops and businesses, it tends to circulate within the community. Local business owners pay taxes locally, employ residents and are more likely to use other local suppliers. This multiplier effect means that a pound spent locally creates more value for the community than the same pound spent at a global chain, which sends its profits overseas.

The environmental argument is equally persuasive. Local businesses generally have shorter supply chains, which means fewer miles travelled and less packaging required. In contrast, the complex global logistics of large corporations contribute significantly to carbon emissions. Choosing local products, particularly food, can therefore be a straightforward way for individuals to reduce their environmental footprint.

In my opinion, the economic argument is the most convincing. Supporting local businesses has a direct and visible impact on the community where one lives, contributing to employment, diversity of services and neighbourhood character. While global chains offer convenience and lower prices, these benefits come at a cost that is often paid by the communities where they operate.
